questions.question: What is the primary function of the 'Sales Linter' for content?

options.text: To check for spelling and grammar errors
To identify and fix 'conversion errors' that prevent content from generating leads
To automatically post your content to social media
To search for keywords that improve SEO

questions.explanation: A sales linter ensures your content is structurally sound for business results, not just grammatical correctness.

questions.question: Where should 'Audience Clarity' be established in a piece of content?

options.text: In the final summary
Within the first 50 words
In the fine print at the bottom of the page
Only in the title

questions.explanation: Modern readers (and AI answer engines) need to know immediately who the content is for. If they have to hunt for relevance, they will leave.

questions.question: What is the recommended 'Value/Ask Balance' for solo founder content?

options.text: 50% Value / 50% Ask
10% Value / 90% Ask
90% Value / 10% Ask
100% Value / 0% Ask

questions.explanation: By over-delivering on value first, you earn the right to make a small, logical 'ask' at the end. Leading with the ask builds resistance; leading with value builds trust.

questions.question: What characterizes a 'Soft CTA'?

options.text: An aggressive demand to 'Buy Now!'
A low-pressure, natural next step that provides more value (e.g., 'Check out this free guide')
A link that doesn't work
A CTA that is hidden and hard to see

questions.explanation: Soft CTAs nurture the relationship without demanding a purchase. They are perfect for in-line placement in TOFU or MOFU content.

questions.question: Why is 'Objection Handling' included in the Sales Linter checklist?

options.text: To argue with people in the comments section
To anticipate and address common hesitations the reader might have before they even reach the CTA
To tell the reader why they are wrong
To explain why your software is the most expensive

questions.explanation: Effective sales content handles objections silently. If you address a pain or cost hesitation early, the reader is much more likely to click the final CTA.

questions.question: Audit your most recent content draft or idea. Give it a score (1-5) on 'Audience Clarity' and 'Soft CTA.' What specific sentence could you add or change to improve these two scores? Why did you pick that specific improvement?
questions.promptForAI: Review if the founder is being objective about their scoring. They should identify a specific 'Fix' (e.g., 'I will change the first sentence to specifically mention CTOs at Series A startups').
